title: terminalia seyrigii
intro: Terminalia seyrigii, also known as the white dammer tree, is a species of plant native to the tropical forests of West and Central Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Terminalia seyrigii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, infusion, and decoction, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
